---
title: "ambient treatment"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
```

```{r}
#read in the dataset
ambient_data <- read.csv2(here("data", "data_ambient_1_081225.csv"))

#rename the datetime column
names(ambient_data)[names(ambient_data) == "X2023.01.30.15.30"] <- "datetime"

#force it to be plaintext first
ambient_data$datetime <- as.character(ambient_data$datetime)

#cleanup weird characters
ambient_data$datetime <- gsub("-", " ", ambient_data$datetime)

#convert to datetime
ambient_data$datetime <- as.POSIXct(
  ambient_data$datetime,
  format = "%Y.%m.%d %H:%M",
  tz = "UTC"
)


#rename the temperature column
ambient_data <- ambient_data %>%
  rename(temperature = X21.06)


```


```{r}
#now filter so that I am only looking at data from the first february 2025. for some reason, the temperatures in january seem way too high
#was the thermologger used for something else in january? was it indoors?
ambient_data_feb2025 <- ambient_data %>%
  filter(datetime >= as.POSIXct("2025-02-01 00:00:00", tz = "UTC"))

```

```{r}
#calculate daily mean temperature
daily_means <- ambient_data_feb2025 %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(datetime),
    temperature = as.numeric(temperature)
  ) %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_temperature = mean(temperature, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

```

```{r}
#now plot the daily mean temperature for ambient treatment
ggplot(daily_means, aes(x = date, y = mean_temperature)) +
  geom_line()
```
```{r}
#now i need to calculate the daily mean temperature for the temperature treatments

temperature_treatments <- read.csv(here("data", "mbiols_daily_temperature_data.csv"))
```


```{r}
#to make sure dates are stored as dates, and temperatures as numbers
temperature_treatments <- temperature_treatments %>%
  mutate(
    date_1   = as.Date(date_1, format = "%d/%m/%Y"),
    date_end = as.Date(date_end, format = "%d/%m/%Y"),
    `plus2.5`    = as.numeric(`plus2.5`),
    `plus1.5`    = as.numeric(`plus1.5`),
    `actual.mean` = as.numeric(`actual.mean`),
    `minus1.5`   = as.numeric(`minus1.5`),
    `minus2.5`   = as.numeric(`minus2.5`)
  )

```

```{r}
#calculate the daily mean temperature for each treatment
daily_mean_treatments <- temperature_treatments %>%
  group_by(date_1, date_end) %>%
  summarise(
    plus2.5_mean  = mean(`plus2.5`, na.rm = TRUE),
    plus1.5_mean  = mean(`plus1.5`, na.rm = TRUE),
    actual_mean   = mean(`actual.mean`, na.rm = TRUE),
    minus1.5_mean = mean(`minus1.5`, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#now combine date_1 and date_end into one column, with one row per date
daily_mean_treatments_long <- daily_mean_treatments %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(date_1, date_end),
    names_to = "date_type",
    values_to = "date"
  ) %>%
  select(-date_type)


# Convert wide to long so we have treatment and mean_temperature columns
plot_data_clean <- daily_mean_treatments_long %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(plus2.5_mean, plus1.5_mean, actual_mean, minus1.5_mean),
    names_to = "treatment",
    values_to = "mean_temperature"
  ) %>%
  mutate(treatment = factor(treatment, 
                            levels = c("minus1.5_mean", "actual_mean", "plus1.5_mean", "plus2.5_mean", "Ambient_mean")))

plot_data_clean <- plot_data_clean %>%
  slice(1:1324)  # keeps rows 1 through 1324

```



```{r}

# Prepare the ambient overlay by adding a "treatment" column
daily_means <- daily_means %>%
  mutate(treatment = "Ambient_mean")  # give it a name for the legend

# Ensure the treatment factor has the correct order
plot_data_clean <- plot_data_clean %>%
  mutate(treatment = factor(treatment, 
                            levels = c("plus2.5_mean", "plus1.5_mean", "actual_mean", "minus1.5_mean", "Ambient_mean")))

daily_means <- daily_means %>%
  mutate(treatment = factor(treatment, 
                            levels = c("plus2.5_mean", "plus1.5_mean", "actual_mean", "minus1.5_mean", "Ambient_mean")))

# Combine plots
plot_1 <- ggplot() +
  # Plot the mean temperature by treatment (thicker lines)
  geom_line(data = plot_data_clean, 
            aes(x = date, y = mean_temperature, color = treatment),
            size = 1.2) +  # increase line thickness for treatments
  # Overlay the daily mean temperature for ambient treatment (dashed, thinner)
  geom_line(data = daily_means, 
            aes(x = date, y = mean_temperature, color = treatment),
            size = 0.6) +   # thinner line for ambient
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "minus1.5_mean" = "#76C0CC",
      "actual_mean"   = "#F9CA0F",
      "plus1.5_mean"  = "#E87A36",
      "plus2.5_mean"  = "#AF0808",
      "Ambient_mean"  = "#484A4C"
    ),
    labels = c(
      "minus1.5_mean" = "cool",
      "actual_mean"   = "mean",
      "plus1.5_mean"  = "warm",
      "plus2.5_mean"  = "hot",
      "Ambient_mean"  = "ambient"
    ),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_labels = "%b",        # only month abbreviation
    date_breaks = "1 month",
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.01))
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Mean daily temperature (°C)",
    title = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    text = element_text(size = 16),          # base size for all text
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18),    # axis titles
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),     # tick labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16),  # legend title
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),   # legend entries
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20)
  )

plot_1

```
```{r}
ggsave("temperature treatments.png", plot = plot_1, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

 

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Filter for October 1, 2025 onwards
plot_data_oct_onwards <- plot_data_clean %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2025-10-01"))

daily_means_oct_onwards <- daily_means %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2025-10-01"))

# Combine plots
ggplot() +
  # Plot the mean temperature by treatment (thicker lines)
  geom_line(data = plot_data_oct_onwards, 
            aes(x = date, y = mean_temperature, color = treatment),
            size = 1.2) +
  # Overlay the daily mean temperature for ambient treatment (dashed, thinner)
  geom_line(data = daily_means_oct_onwards, 
            aes(x = date, y = mean_temperature, color = treatment),
            linetype = "dashed",
            size = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "minus1.5_mean" = "#76C0CC",
      "actual_mean"   = "#F9CA0F",
      "plus1.5_mean"  = "#E87A36",
      "plus2.5_mean"  = "#AF0808",
      "Ambient_mean"  = "black"
    ),
    labels = c(
      "minus1.5_mean" = "cool",
      "actual_mean"   = "mean",
      "plus1.5_mean"  = "warm",
      "plus2.5_mean"  = "hot",
      "Ambient_mean"  = "ambient"
    ),
    name = "Treatment"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_labels = "%b %d",        # month and day
    date_breaks = "1 week",       # weekly breaks for clarity
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.01))
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Date",
    y = "Mean daily temperature (°C)",
    title = "Mean daily temperature by treatment (October 2025 onwards)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()




```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Prepare the ambient overlay by adding a "treatment" column
daily_means <- daily_means %>%
  mutate(treatment = "Ambient_mean")  # give it a name for the legend

# Ensure the treatment factor has the correct order
plot_data_clean <- plot_data_clean %>%
  mutate(treatment = factor(treatment, 
                            levels = c("minus1.5_mean", "actual_mean", "plus1.5_mean", "plus2.5_mean", "Ambient_mean")))

daily_means <- daily_means %>%
  mutate(treatment = factor(treatment, 
                            levels = c("minus1.5_mean", "actual_mean", "plus1.5_mean", "plus2.5_mean", "Ambient_mean")))

# Combine plots
ggplot() +
  # Plot the mean temperature by treatment (thicker lines)
  geom_line(data = plot_data_clean, 
            aes(x = date, y = mean_temperature, color = treatment),
            size = 1.2) +  # increase line thickness for treatments
  # Overlay the daily mean temperature for ambient treatment (dashed, thinner)
  geom_line(data = daily_means, 
            aes(x = date, y = mean_temperature, color = treatment),
            linetype = "dashed",
            size = 0.8) +   # thinner line for ambient
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "minus1.5_mean" = "#76C0CC",
      "actual_mean"   = "#F9CA0F",
      "plus1.5_mean"  = "#E87A36",
      "plus2.5_mean"  = "#AF0808",
      "Ambient_mean"  = "black"
    ),
    labels = c(
      "minus1.5_mean" = "cool",
      "actual_mean"   = "mean",
      "plus1.5_mean"  = "warm",
      "plus2.5_mean"  = "hot",
      "Ambient_mean"  = "ambient"
    ),
    name = "Treatment"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_labels = "%b",        # only month abbreviation
    date_breaks = "1 month",
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.01))
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Date",
    y = "Mean daily temperature (°C)",
    title = "Mean daily temperature by treatment with Ambient overlay"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```
